signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 159.08; 160.00 remains the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. The 450bp Fed-BoJ carry differential supports USD. Risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — watch 157.00 as the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.
evidence: JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (week ending 2026-04-14)

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD LF net long +41,675 (week ending 2026-04-14), long%=72.0% — extreme long positioning. HV30 at 7.1%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 8.3% — options pricing a 1.2 vol-pt premium over realized. AUD/USD at 0.7147; retail sentiment 12% long / 88% short, extreme short bias. Late-trend caution signal — watch for LF delta turning negative or volatility expansion.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 4.50% vs Fed 3.75% — 75bp GBP carry premium supports the pair structurally. GBP LF net long +28,426, AM constructive — positioning aligned with the carry read. GBP/USD at 1.3493; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Inflation + Technical
signals.text: New Zealand inflation at 3.1% year-on-year — NZD supported. NZD/USD at 0.5900; 0.6000 is the next resistance on a continuation. RBNZ rate hike bets increase — watch for further NZD gains.
